title: Workings of a berserk Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Workings of a berserk Evangelion             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Thu Apr 30, 2009 12:53 pm It seems to be a general assumption that the resident souls inside the Evangelions have complete access to the beings functions: that when an Eva goes berserk and the soul inside it seems to be in control, it is in complete charge, being able to influence the Eva at will. However, i think that the whole picture is more complicated than that. I theorize that the dynamics of control exerted by a pilot and by the resident soul could be viewed as analogous to a conscious/instinctual dynamic: the pilot would be the conscious, with a direct control over the Eva's movements, fine motor skills, etc., but with a limited ability in influencing the being, while the resident soul would be the instinctual, with a stronger influence over the Eva's actions, however only being able to ignite primitive, animal like behaviors in the creature. The basis of my argument lies in the many instances Eva-01 went fully berserk: it was always behaving in a very primordial way, and particularly during the Zeruel incident, it's behavior could be described as outright feral, killing the Angel with such brutality, then savagely devouring it's core in a manner that is outright inhuman (it even goes down on all fours). But on other occasions as well, Eva-01 was behaving like a beast spurred by basic instincts rather then it being controlled consciously controlled by a human being (Yui). The point i'm trying to get to is that, in my opinion, when a resident soul takes over an Evangelion, it does so by triggering primitive impulses in the creature that manifest themselves as the so called "berserk" behavior, rather than the soul gaining conscious control and behaving in a feral way (that is, Yui makes the Eva behave savagely, rather than being a savage herself).And the strength of these impulses would depend on the resident souls capability to manipulate the Eva. And when a pilot syncs with an Eva, he/she half overrides, half harnesses these impulses, by working together with the soul. Thoughts? Am i missing something that breaks this theory in half (by the way, i don't consider Eva 01's rescue of Shinji in Episode 01 an act that would require finer control)? Is it baseless and irrelevant? ______________________________________________________________ This part of my post is a continuation of a previous debate i was having with Rock-Slash in a thread that got locked, but it has some overlap with this thread. Rock-Slash wrote: Even if the remains of Kyoko's soul were added to the main part of her soul in Unit 02 core, why would that part continue being insane and lower the "soul quality" of Kyoko?, the more likely is that it would merge with the main part making a complete SANE soul with the same capabilities of Yui's

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Apr 30, 2009 1:42 pm I'm not so sure the soul necessarily has to do anything consciously, what with the whole "She's awake" thing when Yui nommed Angel and absorbed babby. (They had to do way instain Yui, but they never showed how...) Anyway, I've always liked the theory that the synching connected the baser parts of the pilot and resident souls by the primal aspects, so the berserk stage is the overlapping feral parts of both souls in action.

[]: Re: Workings of a berserk Evangelion             Quote Post by VoidEater » Thu Apr 30, 2009 2:41 pm SaltyJoe wrote: It seems to be a general assumption that the resident souls inside the Evangelions have complete...control...

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Thu Apr 30, 2009 3:51 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Anyway, I've always liked the theory that the synching connected the baser parts of the pilot and resident souls by the primal aspects, so the berserk stage is the overlapping feral parts of both souls in action.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 30, 2009 6:21 pm I never really bought into the idea that the Eva's "animalistic nature" is some element separate from the soul, or whatever. Let me throw a Wall of Text at everyone in an attempt to adequately present my own radical point of view. We're told that Evas are a kind of human being. Sadly, the idea is never fully explored... but it does mean that they must have certain intrinsic qualities that let them qualify for this label, despite being counterfeit versions of ancient aliens (who are also human). On the one hand, "human" can refer to purely physical aspects, but usually there's a bit more to it than that... In NGE, there must be, since "humans that reject human form" (Adam's children) are still "people". We never get to see what would happen if an Eva got a fresh soul from the start and got to live out her life as she pleased. Instead, she gets a "pre-established soul" and is subjected to, essentially, a heavily suppressed existence. This whole element of suppression and imprisonment is perhaps worth keeping in mind when considering just why the Evas tend to act batshit crazy, rather than like rational human beings. Much of what we think of as "human" behavior is the result of social conditioning. It's no secret that even the most civilized person can fall back to "animalistic" behavior under certain conditions. (For some people, it takes very little; others might need to be completely broken first.) We're all animals. That holds for the souls inside the Evas. The potential for becoming a hollering monster is in everybody. (Shinji and Asuka sure don't escape this.) Also note that, considering the Evas are tall, lanky bipeds, crawling around on all fours is probably not "normal" even for the type of humanity that they physically represent... Now, consider. You're locked up inside a body that should be yours, but all sorts of technological bondage keeps you from accessing it with any kind of ease. You're aware of what's happening in the outside world, but you have no meaningful contact with anyone. Well, every one in a while, they stick your kid into you, and if you let them "synchronize" with you, they'll gain access to your body and you'll be slightly less lonely for a little bit, even if they don't really acknowledge your existence. (Maybe over the vast amounts of time that you stand around in your giant refrigerator, you'll also catch wind of the fact that your ex-lover is sleeping with someone else, even though he's supposedly still insanely devoted to you. Fuuuuck.) So in this shitty existence, all you have to live for are those rare moments where you're acting as a "medium" and therefore don't have to be going totally insane with loneliness. This kid is all you have. (Well, and maybe that long-term enigmatic plan of yours that heavily involves the kid in some way.) Plus it's, you know, YOUR kid, back from before you were stuck in this awesomely powerful body you can't even use. But say the kid gets into a spot of trouble while ve's in you, or really close by. Perhaps by sheer force of human willpower you can overcome the technology that's always keeping you down. Though these aren't situations where you're going to act like the nice lady folks used to know. You're a deeply suppressed soul, somebody's been trying to hurt your cub (even if they were really just going after the FAKE MOM BAD they've been presented with), and you're totally amped-up on this god body you scarcely knew you had. It's times like this, you just want to run around, howl, and beat shit up. Or worse. Parts of that are skewed towards the Yui/Kyoko situation for the sake of simplicity. Mostly it's just intended to get people thinking about this whole "berserk" thing a different way. I.e., "the Beast" is essentially Yui et al.'s animalistic selves being drawn out as a result of their existences inside the Evas and the situations they're put into. The "maternal monster" (or "Mama Bear", as it's sometimes said) element is also an important factor that should be kept in mind.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 30, 2009 6:32 pm As far as the Soryu's Last Stand in EoE goes, I find it pretty hard (i.e., next to impossible) to escape the conclusion that something very strange is going on, both before AND after Eva-02 goes berserk. The best I can come up with is that Asuka and Kyoko have formed some kind of weird child-mother "complex", symbolized by the shot of Kyoko taking Asuka's hand (with Asuka's hand turning from Child-Asuka to Pilot-Asuka). Piloting is presented as a form of 'returning to the mother', and Asuka relies upon it for her sense of worth and identity. Superficially, the reunion with Kyoko is presented in a positive light, with Asuka returning to her status as a proud and mighty warrior who has achieved some kind of reconciliation with a tortured past. But this is actually illusory -- Asuka has in actuality "regressed". Rather than being freed, at this moment she has pulled deeper into her Eva than ever before -- that thing to which she has enslaved her identity, the mother from whom she has never achieved true separation. With the "complex" established, Asuka and Kyoko act together. Kyoko's influence is manifest in such ways as the fact that Eva-02 vocalizes (which Evas under traditional pilot control never do) and that more of the Eva's innate powers are brought to the fore (not nearly often enough, but it's still something). Asuka's mind does not seem to have been made subordinate to her mother's, but it's perhaps worth keeping the very real possibility of 'psychological contamination' in mind... As the battle drags on, Asuka increasingly... er... loses her bearings. Ms. "a battle should be neat, with no waste" is, right from the get-go, pleased by the sight of torrents of blood: check out her face as she announces, "Erst!" And she's increasingly immersed in the carnage, to the point that she's practically a berserk Eva herself. If in the future she finds herself trapped inside a bad fanfic, as the lucky soul inside some post-NGE Eva unit, she's pretty much got the whole berserker routine down. Constant shouting/grunting, overly brutal fighting, bugged-out eyes, the whole 40-200 meters. Another bit of weirdness indicating that the mother-child bond has gone someplace strange: When Eva-02's innards are being ripped out, Asuka can feel it. What's strange about this is that the Eva is unpowered while this is happening -- "officially", she shouldn't be synchronized. And, of course, there's the unprecedented level of damage that Asuka receives vicariously during powered synchronization, which probably involves some kind of A.T. Field Voodoo. This indicates that she was getting a lot closer to Mom than she should've been (but obviously not enough to engage in Tangscapades). I wrote a bit of 'speculative fan-fiction' trying to explore just what the hell was going on during these scenes, using Kyoko's batshit insane POV. Apologies for the self-promotion, but it is relevant -- the presentation of the theoretical material is just unusual. For those who don't mind getting their hands (eyes?) dirty, see here (starting with "A voice slowly") and here ("She couldn't move").

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 30, 2009 7:01 pm Hah, I remember those RP thread posts. Good times. Excellent posts, Reichu. For some reason, you explaining this stuff here especially, the bridge theory, makes more sense to me now then that other thread it was discussed in awhile back.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Thu Apr 30, 2009 7:14 pm @Reichu: I'll read - haven't washed my eyes in dirty water for a while. I very much agree with the Asuka/Kyoko analysis. I'm not quite sure about the piloting=return to mother, I kinda lean towards piloting=reconciliation with mom, but perhaps that doesn't go far enough. What happens to Asuka is certainly a fusion - perhaps another point of Asuka's lack of individuation, and counterpoint to Shinji's process. I rather hope that Yui (et al) have some degree of perception when not synced, otherwise the rest of the above scenario is really unavoidable...but the light you shed here leads me to believe that the soul maintains even less sense of identity and ability to articulate it's (presumably) noble heritage than I previously thought. You are making me into a Reichu-clone... EDIT: Yeah, like that. Worth reading!

[]: Re: Workings of a berserk Evangelion             Quote Post by Rock-Slash » Thu Apr 30, 2009 7:57 pm Mmm... a serious discussion thread, do this mean I can't do bad jokes and have to moderate my use of the word "shit"? SaltyJoe wrote: But in the end, all we can present each other are theories. Speculations. Some are more valid than others, but since there won't be an occasion when a sagely, aged Anno will descend from Mt. Fuji, carrying stone tablets with blazing kanji written all over them to the Twelve Tribes of Geekdom, proclaming "And on this tables is the TRUTH! Ye shall have no theories besides those which lie herein!", all we will have is fanwanking. So i wasn't trying to sell my own brand of "truth", i was just presenting possibilities. So no need to get all worked up.

[]: Re: Workings of a berserk Evangelion             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Apr 30, 2009 8:51 pm Rock-Slash wrote: Thats exactly what im trying to suggest because of ep 18, if the Dummy plug could made Unit 01 grumble when she was shredding Unit 03 and if Bardiel could made Unit 03 roar using Toji's body, Asuka with her enormous synch ratio could had unconsciously in that arduous battle made Unit 02 growl.

[]: Re: Workings of a berserk Evangelion             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 30, 2009 9:04 pm @SSD: The Asuka/Kyoko stuff possibly makes more sense this time because I've revised my own premise, so some of the bugs from before are hopefully gone. The original "bridge theory" was mostly about Kyoko using Asuka as a "puppet" or something, but that element isn't really present in the current version. (I.e., Kyoko needs Asuka, but she isn't "using" Asuka.) Rock-Slash wrote: Im a bit temperamental

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Apr 30, 2009 10:21 pm Very interesting things are afoot here! A few questions to consider: If an eva goes berserk does the pilot experience that as well in a sort of psychic backwash? It would explain a lot about Shinji's flashback experience in ep 2, especially the short instrumentally-related clips of psychedelic colors and scribbled 'noise' that proceed it. If the resident soul can go berserk using the pilot as a bridge, can the pilot force the unit to go berserk by bridging in the opposite direction? Consider ep19... How much of 01's berserk behavior might be coming from Shinji who before losing power looked pretty close to flipping out? Might the animalistic behavior of the Eva's be a result of their brain configurations, which act as a physical limitation upon the souls they contain? Just because all angels are human doesn't mean all of them can be considered sentient.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Thu Apr 30, 2009 11:05 pm anyone else notice reichu double posted?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 30, 2009 11:10 pm NemZ wrote: If an eva goes berserk does the pilot experience that as well in a sort of psychic backwash? It would explain a lot about Shinji's flashback experience in ep 2, especially the short instrumentally-related clips of psychedelic colors and scribbled 'noise' that proceed it.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Fri May 01, 2009 4:41 am NemZ wrote: If the resident soul can go berserk using the pilot as a bridge, can the pilot force the unit to go berserk by bridging in the opposite direction? Consider ep19... How much of 01's berserk behavior might be coming from Shinji who before losing power looked pretty close to flipping out?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri May 01, 2009 7:09 am Reichu wrote: Shinji would need to have been actually synchronized for any kind of psychic backwash to happen. We see that the entry plug has to come back online after the incident. Also: Aoba (OFF - radio) "Circuits reconnected." Ibuki (OFF - radio) "Systems recovered. Graph placement normal." Hyuga (OFF - radio) "Pilot life signs confirmed." Woman (OFF) "LCL electrification ratio, restored to normal values." Man (OFF) "LCL pressure, restored to normal values." Woman (OFF) "LCL concentration, restored to normal values." Man (OFF) "Plug depth, restored to normal values."

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Fri May 01, 2009 9:46 am I don't think the whole Eva-01 = Yui deal was meant to be taken as literally as it is in here. I mean yes, she is Eva-01. But the way I interpret it, that refers to her essence . Psychologically she's become an Evangelion, a whole different being from the woman who disappeared ten years ago. The way she acts is completely natural given the kind of creature she is.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri May 01, 2009 11:19 am Reichu wrote: Shinji would need to have been actually synchronized for any kind of psychic backwash to happen. We see that the entry plug has to come back online after the incident.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri May 01, 2009 11:37 am Reichu wrote: Let's not forget that, save for the body proportions and a handful of peculiarities -- cores, multiple pupils, four nostrils, etc... -- Eva anatomy likes to follow Lilin textbook guidelines.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Fri May 01, 2009 11:44 am @ Reichu: I knew there was something that slipped out of my head and was trying to crawl back: it's the "maternal monster" part that crystallizes it all for me. That puts the grosser animalistic aspects into context, and allows me to reconcile the gentle, "less is more" protective hand hovering over Shin with the Angel gobbling. I witnessed a significant "mama bear" moment IRL, maybe that's why it suddenly clicks into place for me. I was curious about the plug-depth aspect, and it was rather a revelation in picking out the plug-depth/sync relationship. Thanks for that, too.

However, i think that the whole picture is more complicated than that.

But on other occasions as well, Eva-01 was behaving like a beast spurred by basic instincts rather then it being controlled consciously controlled by a human being (Yui). The point i'm trying to get to is that, in my opinion, when a resident soul takes over an Evangelion, it does so by triggering primitive impulses in the creature that manifest themselves as the so called "berserk" behavior, rather than the soul gaining conscious control and behaving in a feral way (that is, Yui makes the Eva behave savagely, rather than being a savage herself).And the strength of these impulses would depend on the resident souls capability to manipulate the Eva. []:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Apr 30, 2009 10:21 pm Very interesting things are afoot here! A few questions to consider: If an eva goes berserk does the pilot experience that as well in a sort of psychic backwash? It would explain a lot about Shinji's flashback experience in ep 2, especially the short instrumentally-related clips of psychedelic colors and scribbled 'noise' that proceed it. If the resident soul can go berserk using the pilot as a bridge, can the pilot force the unit to go berserk by bridging in the opposite direction? Consider ep19... How much of 01's berserk behavior might be coming from Shinji who before losing power looked pretty close to flipping out? Might the animalistic behavior of the Eva's be a result of their brain configurations, which act as a physical limitation upon the souls they contain? Just because all angels are human doesn't mean all of them can be considered sentient.
